description: A beta-blocker eye drop that reduces intraocular pressure in glaucoma and ocular hypertension.
howToTake: Instill 1 drop twice daily; press on the inner corner of the eye for 1–2 minutes after to reduce systemic absorption.

description: Lubricating eye drops that supplement natural tears, providing relief for dry eyes and eye strain.
howToTake: Instill 1–2 drops as needed throughout the day; safe for frequent use.

description: Antibiotic ear drops for otitis externa (swimmer's ear) and chronic suppurative otitis media.
howToTake: Warm drops to body temperature; instill 5 drops in the affected ear twice daily for 7–14 days.

description: A nasal corticosteroid spray that reduces nasal inflammation, congestion, and sneezing in allergic rhinitis.
howToTake: 2 sprays in each nostril once daily; aim away from the septum. Takes a few days for full effect.

description: A decongestant nasal spray that shrinks swollen nasal tissues for quick relief of blocked nose. Short-term use only.
howToTake: 2–3 drops or sprays per nostril 2–3 times daily; do NOT use for more than 5–7 days (rebound congestion).

description: Natural progesterone supplement used for luteal phase support, irregular periods, and threatened miscarriage.
howToTake: Take orally at bedtime (causes drowsiness) or insert vaginally as directed by your doctor.

description: A progestogen used to delay periods, treat irregular menstruation, endometriosis, and abnormal uterine bleeding.
howToTake: Take 1–3 tablets daily as directed; to delay periods, start 3 days before expected date.

description: A combined oral contraceptive pill that prevents pregnancy by suppressing ovulation and thickening cervical mucus.
howToTake: Take one pill daily at the same time for 21 days, then 7 days pill-free. Start on day 1 of period.

description: An antifibrinolytic that reduces heavy menstrual bleeding and excessive bleeding during dental procedures or surgeries.
howToTake: Take 2–3 times daily during heavy bleeding days; maximum 5 days per menstrual cycle.

description: A prostaglandin used for NSAID-induced ulcer prevention, cervical ripening, and medical management of miscarriage.
howToTake: For gastric protection: take 4 times daily with food and at bedtime. For obstetric use: as directed by doctor only.

description: A broad-spectrum anthelmintic that kills intestinal worms including roundworm, hookworm, and threadworm.
howToTake: Take as a single dose of 400mg with food; may repeat after 2–3 weeks if needed.

description: An antiparasitic used for strongyloidiasis, onchocerciasis, scabies, and head lice. Also used for some ectoparasites.
howToTake: Take on an empty stomach with water; single or two-dose regimen depending on the condition.

description: A prokinetic and anti-emetic that speeds up stomach emptying and reduces nausea and vomiting.
howToTake: Take 15–30 minutes before meals and at bedtime; do not use for more than 12 weeks.

description: A bile acid that dissolves cholesterol gallstones and protects the liver in conditions like primary biliary cholangitis.
howToTake: Take with food at bedtime; treatment may last months to years depending on the condition.

description: A hepatoprotective supplement derived from milk thistle, used to support liver health and protect against liver damage.
howToTake: Take 2–3 times daily with meals.

description: An anti-secretory anti-diarrhoeal that reduces water and electrolyte loss without slowing gut motility.
howToTake: Take 3 times daily before meals until diarrhoea stops; maximum 7 days.

description: An anti-diarrhoeal that slows intestinal motility, reducing the frequency of loose stools.
howToTake: Take 4mg initially, then 2mg after each loose stool; maximum 16mg per day.

description: A balanced electrolyte and glucose solution for preventing and treating dehydration from diarrhoea and vomiting.
howToTake: Dissolve 1 sachet in 1 litre of clean water; sip frequently. Do not add sugar or salt.

description: Beneficial bacteria and yeast that restore gut flora, used during antibiotic courses and for diarrhoea management.
howToTake: Take 1–2 capsules daily with food, ideally 2 hours after antibiotics.

description: The active form of vitamin D that enhances calcium absorption, used in renal disease and severe vitamin D deficiency.
howToTake: Take once daily with or without food; requires regular calcium level monitoring.

description: A combination of essential B vitamins for nerve health, particularly useful in neuropathy and nutritional deficiencies.
howToTake: Take once daily with food.

description: Essential B vitamin critical for DNA synthesis, red blood cell production, and preventing neural tube defects in pregnancy.
howToTake: Take once daily with or without food; recommended to start before conception and continue through first trimester.

description: A comprehensive combination of essential vitamins and minerals for overall nutritional support and deficiency prevention.
howToTake: Take once daily after a meal; avoid taking with tea or coffee which reduce mineral absorption.

description: A short-acting nitrate used for prevention and treatment of angina; can be placed under the tongue for quick relief.
howToTake: Sublingual: place under tongue during chest pain. Oral: take 2–3 times daily with a nitrate-free interval.

description: A fast-acting nitrate for immediate relief of angina pain; dilates coronary arteries within minutes.
howToTake: Place sublingual tablet under the tongue at first sign of chest pain; sit down before taking. May repeat every 5 minutes up to 3 times.

description: A calcium channel blocker used for hypertension, angina, and certain arrhythmias; slows heart rate unlike amlodipine.
howToTake: Take 3–4 times daily, or once daily for extended-release forms; take before meals.

description: A calcium channel blocker for angina, hypertension, and supraventricular arrhythmias; strong heart rate control.
howToTake: Take 3 times daily with food; extended-release forms taken once daily. Do NOT combine with beta-blockers.

description: A first-in-class ARNI that combines neprilysin inhibition with ARB for heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (HFrEF).
howToTake: Take twice daily with or without food; do not use with ACE inhibitors (36-hour washout needed).

description: Dual antiplatelet therapy for post-stent, acute coronary syndrome, and stroke prevention — standard cardiac combination.
howToTake: Take once daily with or without food; co-prescribe a PPI for stomach protection.

description: A combination of antiplatelet and statin for cardiovascular risk reduction in patients requiring both therapies.
howToTake: Take once daily at bedtime after food.

description: A commonly prescribed combination of insulin sensitizer and insulin secretagogue for better blood sugar control.
howToTake: Take with the first meal of the day; do not skip meals after taking.

description: A combination of DPP-4 inhibitor and metformin for improved glycemic control with lower hypoglycemia risk.
howToTake: Take twice daily with meals.

description: A combination DPP-4 inhibitor with metformin for Type 2 diabetes management with dual mechanism of action.
howToTake: Take twice daily with meals.

description: A combination ARB plus diuretic for hypertension when single-agent therapy is insufficient.
howToTake: Take once daily at the same time; monitor potassium levels.

description: A combination ARB plus diuretic for hypertension, providing complementary blood pressure lowering mechanisms.
howToTake: Take once daily with or without food.

description: A combination ACE inhibitor plus diuretic for hypertension when additional blood pressure lowering is needed.
howToTake: Take once daily; monitor potassium and renal function.

description: Potassium supplement for treating and preventing low potassium, commonly needed with diuretic therapy.
howToTake: Take with food and a full glass of water to reduce stomach upset; do not crush extended-release tablets.

description: A first-generation antihistamine with strong anti-nausea and sedative properties, used for allergies, motion sickness, and sleep.
howToTake: Take at bedtime for allergies/sleep; take 30 minutes before travel for motion sickness.

description: A combination H2 blocker and prokinetic for acidity with nausea and bloating.
howToTake: Take twice daily 30 minutes before meals.

description: A combination of pain reliever and caffeine for headache and migraine; caffeine enhances paracetamol's effect by 40%.
howToTake: Take 1–2 tablets every 4–6 hours as needed; do not exceed 8 tablets per day.

description: A triple combination of NSAID, paracetamol, and enzyme for pain, inflammation, and swelling after surgery or injury.
howToTake: Take twice daily after meals; not recommended for more than 5–7 days.

description: A combination of paracetamol with a weak opioid for moderate to moderately severe pain.
howToTake: Take 1–2 tablets every 4–6 hours; maximum 8 tablets per day.

description: A first-generation antihistamine for allergic rhinitis, urticaria, and common cold symptoms; causes drowsiness.
howToTake: Take 3–4 times daily; can be taken at bedtime if drowsiness is bothersome.

description: A first-generation antihistamine also used as a sleep aid and for motion sickness due to its sedating properties.
howToTake: For allergies: 25–50mg every 6–8 hours. For sleep: 25–50mg at bedtime.

description: A combination of anti-emetic and pain reliever for headache with nausea, particularly migraine-associated nausea.
howToTake: Take 1 tablet at onset of nausea/headache; may repeat after 4–6 hours if needed.

description: A combination of nerve pain reliever with B12 for diabetic neuropathy, post-herpetic neuralgia, and nerve damage.
howToTake: Take twice daily with or without food.

description: A natural hormone supplement that regulates sleep-wake cycles, used for insomnia, jet lag, and delayed sleep phase.
howToTake: Take 30–60 minutes before bedtime; start with the lowest dose. Do not use with alcohol.

description: A non-benzodiazepine hypnotic for short-term treatment of insomnia; works on GABA receptors selectively for sleep.
howToTake: Take immediately before bedtime on an empty stomach; allow 7–8 hours for sleep.

description: A powerful third-generation cephalosporin antibiotic given by injection for serious infections like meningitis, pneumonia, and sepsis.
howToTake: Given as an intramuscular or intravenous injection by a healthcare professional.

description: A third-generation cephalosporin injectable antibiotic used for severe bacterial infections including meningitis and surgical prophylaxis.
howToTake: Administered intravenously or intramuscularly by healthcare professionals.

description: A broad-spectrum carbapenem antibiotic reserved for serious multi-drug resistant infections in hospital settings.
howToTake: Given by intravenous infusion in a hospital setting every 8 hours.

description: A powerful combination antibiotic for severe hospital-acquired infections. Tazobactam protects piperacillin from bacterial resistance enzymes.
howToTake: Given by IV infusion every 6-8 hours in a hospital setting.

description: A combination antibiotic effective against UTIs, ear infections, bronchitis, and traveler's diarrhoea.
howToTake: Take twice daily with a full glass of water; stay well hydrated during treatment.

description: An aminoglycoside antibiotic for serious gram-negative infections. Requires careful monitoring of kidney function and hearing.
howToTake: Given by injection in a hospital; dose is calculated based on body weight and kidney function.

description: A potent aminoglycoside antibiotic used for serious infections resistant to gentamicin, especially in hospital settings.
howToTake: Given by intramuscular or intravenous injection; dose based on weight and kidney function.

description: A glycopeptide antibiotic reserved for serious MRSA and resistant gram-positive infections. Often called the antibiotic of last resort.
howToTake: Given by slow IV infusion over at least 60 minutes; rapid infusion causes 'red man syndrome'.

description: A key anti-tuberculosis antibiotic that kills TB bacteria by blocking RNA synthesis. Also used for leprosy and some other infections.
howToTake: Take on an empty stomach 30 minutes before or 2 hours after meals; turns urine/tears orange-red (harmless).

description: The most important first-line anti-TB drug that kills actively growing TB bacteria. Usually given with pyridoxine (B6) to prevent nerve damage.
howToTake: Take on an empty stomach; always take vitamin B6 supplement alongside to prevent nerve side effects.

description: A first-line anti-TB drug that works in acidic environments inside cells, especially effective during the intensive phase of TB treatment.
howToTake: Take once daily with food; report any joint pain or dark-coloured urine to your doctor.

description: A first-line anti-TB drug that prevents bacterial cell wall formation. Used in combination therapy to prevent resistance.
howToTake: Take once daily with food; regular eye examinations are essential as it can affect vision.

description: A fixed-dose combination of all four first-line anti-TB drugs used during the intensive phase (first 2 months) of tuberculosis treatment.
howToTake: Take on an empty stomach at the same time daily; take vitamin B6 supplement alongside. Complete the full course (6-9 months).

description: An oral penem antibiotic used for moderate respiratory, urinary, and ENT infections when other antibiotics have failed.
howToTake: Take twice daily with food; complete the full course as prescribed.

description: An SGLT2 inhibitor that lowers blood sugar by causing the kidneys to excrete excess glucose in urine. Also protects heart and kidneys.
howToTake: Take once daily before the first meal of the day; drink plenty of water to prevent UTIs.

description: A sulfonylurea that stimulates insulin release from the pancreas, used for Type 2 diabetes when metformin alone is insufficient.
howToTake: Take 30 minutes before meals; do not skip meals after taking to avoid hypoglycemia.

description: An alpha-glucosidase inhibitor that slows carbohydrate digestion, reducing post-meal blood sugar spikes.
howToTake: Chew with the first bite of each main meal; start with a low dose to reduce GI side effects.

description: An intermediate-acting insulin that covers blood sugar between meals and overnight. Usually given twice daily.
howToTake: Inject subcutaneously; gently roll (don't shake) the vial before use. Rotate injection sites.

description: A rapid-acting insulin analog that starts working within 15 minutes of injection, covering mealtime blood sugar rises.
howToTake: Inject within 15 minutes before eating; do not use if solution is cloudy or has particles.

description: A premixed insulin containing 30% rapid-acting and 70% intermediate-acting insulin, providing both mealtime and background coverage.
howToTake: Inject 15-30 minutes before meals; gently roll the pen/vial to mix. Usually given twice daily.

description: A combination of metformin and teneligliptin for better blood sugar control through complementary mechanisms — reducing glucose production and increasing insulin release.
howToTake: Take with meals; swallow whole without crushing.

description: Combines metformin's glucose-lowering action with dapagliflozin's kidney-based glucose excretion for superior diabetes control with heart and kidney benefits.
howToTake: Take once daily with breakfast; drink plenty of water throughout the day.

description: A newer antiplatelet drug used after heart attacks and stent placement. Works faster and more predictably than clopidogrel.
howToTake: Take twice daily with or without food; do not stop without doctor's advice.

description: A direct thrombin inhibitor (NOAC) that prevents blood clots in atrial fibrillation and after joint replacement surgery.
howToTake: Swallow capsules whole — never open, crush, or chew. Take twice daily at the same times.

description: A low molecular weight heparin injected subcutaneously to prevent and treat deep vein thrombosis and pulmonary embolism.
howToTake: Inject subcutaneously into the abdomen; alternate injection sites. Do not rub the injection site.

description: A dual L/N-type calcium channel blocker that lowers blood pressure with less ankle swelling and reflex heart rate increase than amlodipine.
howToTake: Take once daily with or without food at a consistent time.

description: An alpha-1 blocker that relaxes blood vessels to lower blood pressure. Also used for Raynaud's disease and BPH symptoms.
howToTake: Start with a low dose at bedtime (first-dose hypotension risk); take 2-3 times daily once stabilized.

description: A thiazide-like diuretic with longer duration of action than hydrochlorothiazide, used as first-line therapy for hypertension.
howToTake: Take once daily in the morning; monitor potassium levels regularly.

description: A thiazide-like diuretic that lowers blood pressure with less metabolic disturbance (less effect on sugar and potassium) compared to older diuretics.
howToTake: Take once daily in the morning; swallow sustained-release tablets whole.

description: An ACE inhibitor that protects the heart and kidneys while lowering blood pressure. Shown to reduce cardiovascular events in clinical trials.
howToTake: Take once daily in the morning before food; avoid potassium supplements.

description: A potassium channel opener with nitrate-like action that widens coronary arteries, used for stable angina prevention.
howToTake: Take twice daily; may cause headache initially which improves with continued use.

description: A heart rate-lowering drug that works by specifically slowing the heart's pacemaker. Used for angina and heart failure when beta-blockers aren't enough.
howToTake: Take twice daily with meals; avoid grapefruit juice.

description: A metabolic agent that improves heart muscle energy efficiency by shifting fuel use from fatty acids to glucose, reducing angina episodes.
howToTake: Take twice daily with meals; MR tablets should be swallowed whole.

description: An anti-anginal drug that works by a unique mechanism — blocking late sodium current in heart cells — to reduce angina without lowering heart rate or blood pressure.
howToTake: Take twice daily with or without food; swallow extended-release tablets whole.

description: A potent antiplatelet drug used after heart attacks and stenting. Works faster and more consistently than clopidogrel but with higher bleeding risk.
howToTake: Take once daily with or without food; usually started with a 60mg loading dose in hospital.

description: A methylxanthine bronchodilator that relaxes airway muscles and reduces inflammation in asthma and COPD. Has a narrow therapeutic window.
howToTake: Take at the same time daily; swallow sustained-release tablets whole. Avoid caffeine excess.

description: An anticholinergic bronchodilator that opens airways by blocking vagal nerve signals. Often used alongside salbutamol for COPD and severe asthma.
howToTake: Inhale as directed, usually 3-4 times daily; can be nebulised with salbutamol for acute attacks.

description: An inhaled corticosteroid that reduces airway inflammation for long-term asthma control. One of the oldest and most proven inhaled steroids.
howToTake: Use regularly as prescribed (not for rescue); rinse mouth after each use to prevent thrush.

description: A newer methylxanthine bronchodilator with fewer cardiac side effects and a wider safety margin compared to theophylline.
howToTake: Take twice daily with or after food; does not require blood level monitoring unlike theophylline.

description: A mucolytic that breaks down thick mucus in respiratory conditions. Also used as an antidote for paracetamol overdose and to protect the liver.
howToTake: Take 2-3 times daily; dissolve effervescent tablets in water. Drink plenty of fluids.

description: A combination of a leukotriene antagonist and a non-drowsy antihistamine for comprehensive allergy and asthma symptom relief.
howToTake: Take once daily in the evening; can be taken with or without food.

description: The S-isomer of omeprazole — a proton pump inhibitor that may provide slightly faster acid suppression than omeprazole.
howToTake: Take 30-60 minutes before meals on an empty stomach; swallow whole without crushing.

description: An H2 blocker that reduces stomach acid production, used for heartburn, peptic ulcers, and GERD. Preferred H2 blocker after ranitidine withdrawal concerns.
howToTake: Take once or twice daily with or without food; can take 15-60 minutes before meals to prevent heartburn.

description: Combines a PPI to reduce acid with a prokinetic to speed up stomach emptying — used for GERD with nausea or bloating.
howToTake: Take 30 minutes before breakfast; swallow the capsule whole.

description: A classic antacid combination that neutralizes excess stomach acid. Aluminium prevents diarrhoea from magnesium, and magnesium prevents constipation from aluminium.
howToTake: Take 1-2 teaspoons between meals and at bedtime; shake the bottle well before use.

description: Sucralfate coats and protects stomach ulcers while oxetacaine provides immediate pain relief by numbing the stomach lining.
howToTake: Take on an empty stomach 1 hour before meals; shake well before use.

description: A COX-2 preferential NSAID used for pain and inflammation. Banned in some countries due to liver toxicity risk but widely used in India.
howToTake: Take twice daily after meals; avoid in liver disease; limit use to 15 days maximum.

description: A selective COX-2 inhibitor that provides anti-inflammatory and pain relief with less stomach irritation than traditional NSAIDs.
howToTake: Take once or twice daily with or without food; use the lowest effective dose for the shortest time.

description: A popular combination of two pain relievers that work by different mechanisms, providing better pain relief than either alone.
howToTake: Take after meals every 6-8 hours; do not exceed 3 tablets per day.

description: A dual-action opioid painkiller for moderate to severe pain. Less constipation and nausea compared to tramadol and morphine.
howToTake: Take every 4-6 hours as needed; do not exceed prescribed dose. Extended-release: swallow whole, do not crush.

description: An NSAID used for dental pain, sore throat, and musculoskeletal conditions. Available as tablets and as lozenges for sore throat.
howToTake: Take 2-3 times daily with food; for sore throat, dissolve lozenge slowly in mouth.

description: An SSRI antidepressant used for depression, anxiety disorders, OCD, PTSD, and panic disorder. Has the strongest anxiolytic effect among SSRIs.
howToTake: Take once daily in the morning with food; do not stop abruptly — taper gradually.

description: An SNRI antidepressant that increases both serotonin and norepinephrine. Used for depression, anxiety, neuropathic pain, and hot flashes.
howToTake: Take once daily with food; swallow XR capsules whole. Do not stop abruptly — discontinuation syndrome is common.

description: The active metabolite of venlafaxine — an SNRI with more predictable blood levels and fewer drug interactions than its parent compound.
howToTake: Take once daily with or without food; swallow whole. Do not stop abruptly.

description: An atypical antipsychotic with unique partial dopamine agonist action. Causes less weight gain and metabolic effects than other antipsychotics.
howToTake: Take once daily with or without food; dose is gradually adjusted by your doctor.

description: A unique antidepressant that works on dopamine and norepinephrine. Does not cause weight gain or sexual dysfunction. Also used for smoking cessation.
howToTake: Take once or twice daily; do not take close to bedtime (insomnia). Swallow SR/XL tablets whole.

description: An antidepressant commonly used at low doses as a sleep aid. Works on serotonin receptors without the sexual side effects of SSRIs.
howToTake: Take at bedtime for insomnia or 2-3 times daily for depression; take with food.

description: The active metabolite of risperidone with a more predictable blood level profile. Used for schizophrenia and schizoaffective disorder.
howToTake: Take once daily in the morning with or without food; swallow whole, do not crush.

description: An SSRI primarily used for OCD (obsessive-compulsive disorder). Also effective for social anxiety disorder and depression.
howToTake: Take once daily at bedtime to minimize nausea; increase dose gradually as directed.

description: An anticonvulsant used for epilepsy and migraine prevention. Causes weight loss unlike most anti-epileptics.
howToTake: Take twice daily with or without food; drink plenty of water to prevent kidney stones. Start at a low dose.

description: An anticonvulsant related to carbamazepine but with fewer drug interactions and better tolerability. Used for epilepsy and trigeminal neuralgia.
howToTake: Take twice daily with or without food; monitor sodium levels especially when starting.

description: An anticonvulsant and mood stabilizer used for epilepsy and bipolar disorder. Must be started very slowly to avoid serious skin reactions.
howToTake: Start with a very low dose and increase gradually over weeks. Take with or without food.

description: A cholinesterase inhibitor that improves memory and cognition in mild to moderate Alzheimer's disease by increasing acetylcholine in the brain.
howToTake: Take once daily at bedtime with or without food; start at 5mg for at least 4-6 weeks before increasing.

description: An NMDA receptor antagonist used for moderate to severe Alzheimer's disease. Works differently from donepezil and can be combined with it.
howToTake: Take once or twice daily with or without food; dose is increased gradually over weeks.

description: A dopamine agonist used for Parkinson's disease and restless legs syndrome. Stimulates dopamine receptors directly.
howToTake: Take three times daily with or without food; dose is increased very gradually. Avoid sudden discontinuation.

description: A vitamin A derivative (retinoid) that treats acne by unclogging pores and promoting skin cell turnover. Also reduces fine wrinkles and sun damage.
howToTake: Apply a thin layer at bedtime on dry skin; start 2-3 times per week. Use sunscreen daily — increases sun sensitivity.

description: An antibacterial and keratolytic agent for acne. Kills acne-causing bacteria and unclogs pores without causing antibiotic resistance.
howToTake: Apply once or twice daily after washing face; start with 2.5% to minimize irritation. May bleach fabric.

description: A mild topical corticosteroid for eczema, dermatitis, insect bites, and minor skin irritations. Safe for use on the face for short periods.
howToTake: Apply a thin layer 1-2 times daily; do not use on open wounds. Avoid prolonged use on the face.

description: A topical immunomodulator for eczema that reduces inflammation without the skin-thinning effects of steroids. Preferred for face and sensitive areas.
howToTake: Apply a thin layer twice daily to affected areas; may cause temporary burning sensation initially.

description: A topical antimicrobial cream used primarily for burn wounds to prevent and treat infections. Releases silver ions that kill bacteria.
howToTake: Apply a thick layer (1-2mm) to the burn/wound 1-2 times daily after cleaning. Cover with a sterile dressing.

description: A topical antifungal for ringworm, athlete's foot, jock itch, and skin candidiasis. Also available as an oral gel for mouth thrush.
howToTake: Apply twice daily to clean, dry affected skin; continue for 1-2 weeks after symptoms resolve.

description: A keratolytic agent that softens and removes dead skin. Used for acne, warts, psoriasis, dandruff, and calluses.
howToTake: Apply to affected area; for warts use higher concentration with precision. Avoid on healthy skin, moles, or birthmarks.

description: A vitamin D analog for psoriasis that slows skin cell overgrowth and reduces scaling without the side effects of steroids.
howToTake: Apply twice daily to psoriasis patches; do not exceed 100g per week. Avoid face and skin folds.

description: An alpha-2 agonist eye drop that lowers eye pressure in glaucoma by reducing fluid production and increasing drainage.
howToTake: Instill 1 drop in affected eye(s) 2-3 times daily; press on inner corner of eye after instilling to prevent systemic absorption.

description: A prostaglandin analog that lowers eye pressure by increasing fluid outflow. The most commonly prescribed first-line glaucoma drop.
howToTake: Instill 1 drop in each affected eye once daily in the evening; may darken iris colour permanently.

description: A carbonic anhydrase inhibitor eye drop that lowers eye pressure by reducing aqueous humor production. Used for glaucoma.
howToTake: Instill 1 drop 2-3 times daily; can be used alone or with other glaucoma drops.

description: A fluoroquinolone antibiotic eye drop for bacterial conjunctivitis, corneal ulcers, and post-operative eye infection prevention.
howToTake: Instill 1-2 drops every 2-4 hours initially, then reduce to 4 times daily. Complete the full course.

description: An aminoglycoside antibiotic eye drop effective against a broad range of eye infections, particularly those caused by gram-negative bacteria.
howToTake: Instill 1-2 drops every 4-6 hours; in severe infections, may be used every hour initially.

description: A corticosteroid eye drop that reduces eye inflammation, swelling, and redness after surgery, injury, or in conditions like uveitis and allergic conjunctivitis.
howToTake: Instill 1-2 drops 4-6 times daily; taper gradually — do not stop abruptly after prolonged use.

description: An NSAID eye drop used to reduce pain and inflammation after cataract surgery. Also helps prevent macular oedema.
howToTake: Instill 1 drop 3 times daily starting 1 day before surgery and continuing for 2-4 weeks after.

description: Calcium citrate is better absorbed than calcium carbonate, especially in elderly and those on acid-reducing medications. Combined with D3 for optimal absorption.
howToTake: Take with or without food (advantage over calcium carbonate); split doses for better absorption.

description: An active vitamin D analog that doesn't require kidney conversion. Used for patients with kidney disease, hypoparathyroidism, and osteoporosis.
howToTake: Take once daily; dose adjusted based on calcium and phosphorus blood levels.

description: A fat-soluble antioxidant vitamin that protects cells from oxidative damage. Used for vitamin E deficiency and skin health.
howToTake: Take with a fatty meal for better absorption; do not exceed recommended dose.

description: A powerful antioxidant that helps nerve function and blood sugar control. Used as an adjunct for diabetic neuropathy.
howToTake: Take on an empty stomach for better absorption; usually taken once or twice daily.

description: Vitamin B6 supports nerve function and red blood cell formation. Essential supplement with isoniazid (TB drug) to prevent nerve damage.
howToTake: Take once daily with food; essential co-supplement during TB treatment with isoniazid.

description: A B-vitamin (B7) that supports hair, skin, and nail health. Popular supplement for hair loss and brittle nails.
howToTake: Take once daily with or without food; results may take 3-6 months to become visible.

description: An iron supplement containing a well-absorbed form of iron. Used to treat and prevent iron deficiency anemia.
howToTake: Take on an empty stomach with vitamin C (juice) for better absorption; avoid tea, coffee, and calcium around dose time.

description: A centrally acting muscle relaxant that reduces spasticity in conditions like multiple sclerosis, spinal cord injuries, and cerebral palsy.
howToTake: Take 3 times daily with food; start low and increase gradually. Do not stop abruptly.

description: A muscle relaxant used for acute musculoskeletal pain and spasms. Causes less drowsiness than many other muscle relaxants.
howToTake: Take 3-4 times daily with or without food; best used for short-term relief (2-3 weeks).

description: An aromatase inhibitor used for breast cancer in postmenopausal women and for ovulation induction in infertility treatment.
howToTake: Take once daily with or without food; for infertility, take on specific cycle days as directed by your doctor.

description: A selective estrogen receptor modulator (SERM) that stimulates ovulation. First-line treatment for anovulatory infertility (PCOS).
howToTake: Take for 5 days starting on cycle day 2-5 as directed by your gynecologist; monitoring with ultrasound is recommended.

description: An intravenous iron preparation for severe iron deficiency anemia when oral iron is not tolerated or insufficient, especially in pregnancy and kidney disease.
howToTake: Administered by slow IV infusion in a clinic or hospital; typically 200mg per session, 2-3 sessions per week.

description: A progesterone receptor blocker used for medical termination of early pregnancy (up to 63 days) when combined with misoprostol. Strictly regulated.
howToTake: Must be taken under medical supervision only; followed by misoprostol 24-48 hours later as directed by the doctor.

description: A first-generation antihistamine used for anxiety, itching, allergies, and as a pre-operative sedative. Provides both allergy relief and calming effects.
howToTake: Take 2-3 times daily or at bedtime for anxiety/insomnia; causes significant drowsiness.

description: An antihistamine with antiserotonin properties. Used for allergies but widely prescribed in India as an appetite stimulant, especially for underweight children.
howToTake: Take 2-3 times daily before meals; commonly given to children to stimulate appetite.

description: An antibiotic primarily used for leprosy (multi-drug therapy) and dermatitis herpetiformis. Also used in some autoimmune skin conditions.
howToTake: Take once daily with food; regular blood tests are needed to monitor for anemia and other side effects.

description: A dopamine agonist used to treat high prolactin levels, prolactinoma (pituitary tumor), and to suppress lactation when medically needed.
howToTake: Take twice weekly with food; dose is adjusted based on prolactin blood levels.

description: A dopamine agonist used for hyperprolactinemia, Parkinson's disease, acromegaly, and lactation suppression.
howToTake: Take with food at bedtime initially to reduce nausea; increase dose gradually as directed.

description: A synthetic ADH analog used for diabetes insipidus, bedwetting in children, and to control bleeding in certain clotting disorders.
howToTake: Take 2-3 times daily; restrict fluid intake to prevent dangerous low sodium levels.

description: An NK1 receptor antagonist used to prevent chemotherapy-induced nausea and vomiting. Added to standard antiemetics for highly emetogenic chemo.
howToTake: Take 125mg on day 1 (before chemo) and 80mg on days 2 and 3.

description: A 5-HT3 receptor antagonist that prevents nausea and vomiting from chemotherapy, radiation therapy, and post-operative recovery.
howToTake: Take 1mg twice daily or 2mg once daily; first dose 30 minutes before chemotherapy.

description: A white blood cell growth factor (G-CSF) used to boost immune recovery after chemotherapy and for severe neutropenia.
howToTake: Injected subcutaneously once daily, starting 24 hours after chemo, until white cell count recovers.

description: A dual 5-alpha reductase inhibitor for benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH). Shrinks the prostate more effectively than finasteride by blocking both enzyme types.
howToTake: Take once daily with or without food; swallow capsules whole. Takes 3-6 months for full effect.

description: An alpha-1 blocker that relaxes prostate and bladder neck muscles, improving urine flow in BPH. Causes less ejaculatory problems than tamsulosin.
howToTake: Take once daily after the same meal each day; swallow extended-release tablets whole.

description: An anticholinergic drug for overactive bladder syndrome — reduces urgency, frequency, and urge incontinence.
howToTake: Take once daily with or without food; swallow whole with water.

description: A common cold relief combination: antihistamine for runny nose, decongestant for blocked nose, and paracetamol for fever and body ache.
howToTake: Take every 6-8 hours as needed; do not exceed 4 tablets per day. Causes drowsiness.

description: A cough and cold syrup combining an antihistamine, decongestant, and cough suppressant for dry cough with cold symptoms.
howToTake: Take 10ml every 6-8 hours; avoid driving due to drowsiness. Do not use for productive (phlegmy) cough.

description: A triple-action cough syrup: ambroxol thins mucus, guaifenesin helps expel it, and terbutaline opens airways for easier breathing.
howToTake: Take 10ml 2-3 times daily; suitable for productive (wet) cough with wheezing.

description: A combination antibiotic where clavulanate extends cefpodoxime's activity against resistant bacteria. Used for respiratory and urinary infections.
howToTake: Take twice daily with food for better absorption; complete the full prescribed course.

description: A combination antibiotic for mixed infections: ofloxacin covers bacteria while ornidazole covers anaerobic bacteria and parasites. Common for diarrhoea and dental infections.
howToTake: Take twice daily after meals; avoid alcohol during treatment and for 3 days after.

description: India's most widely used combination for dysentery and diarrhoea: norfloxacin kills bacteria while tinidazole kills parasites like Giardia and amoeba.
howToTake: Take twice daily on an empty stomach with plenty of water; avoid alcohol during treatment.

description: A second-generation cephalosporin boosted with clavulanate for resistance coverage. Used for moderate respiratory and urinary tract infections.
howToTake: Take twice daily with food for better absorption; complete the full prescribed course.

description: A corticosteroid nasal spray for allergic rhinitis, nasal polyps, and sinusitis. Reduces inflammation, congestion, and sneezing.
howToTake: Spray 1-2 puffs in each nostril once daily; may take a few days for full effect.

description: An antihistamine nasal spray that provides fast relief from allergic rhinitis symptoms. Works within 15 minutes — faster than oral antihistamines.
howToTake: Spray 1-2 puffs in each nostril twice daily; prime the pump before first use.

description: A decongestant nasal drop safe for infants and children. Shrinks swollen nasal passages for easier breathing.
howToTake: Instill 1-2 drops in each nostril; do not use for more than 3-5 days to avoid rebound congestion.

description: An alcohol deterrent drug that causes extremely unpleasant reactions when alcohol is consumed, used as part of alcohol de-addiction programs.
howToTake: Take once daily in the morning; must completely avoid ALL alcohol including hidden sources (mouthwash, sauces, medications).

description: Nicotine replacement therapy (gum, patches, lozenges) that helps quit smoking by providing controlled nicotine doses without harmful tobacco chemicals.
howToTake: Gum: chew slowly, park between cheek and gum. Patch: apply to clean, dry skin daily. Reduce dose gradually over 8-12 weeks.

description: A newer combination replacing telmisartan + HCTZ. Chlorthalidone provides stronger 24-hour BP control than hydrochlorothiazide.
howToTake: Take once daily in the morning; monitor potassium and kidney function periodically.

description: A widely prescribed combination of calcium channel blocker and beta-blocker for blood pressure control, especially when either alone is insufficient.
howToTake: Take once daily with or without food; monitor heart rate and blood pressure regularly.

description: A combination targeting both fasting and post-meal blood sugar: metformin controls fasting glucose while voglibose slows carbohydrate digestion.
howToTake: Take with the first bite of each meal; start with a low dose to minimize GI side effects.

description: A triple diabetes combination targeting insulin release (glimepiride), glucose production (metformin), and post-meal spikes (voglibose).
howToTake: Take with the first meal of the day; never skip meals after taking to prevent hypoglycemia.

description: A cardiovascular polypill combining cholesterol lowering (statin) with dual antiplatelet therapy — a standard combination after heart attacks and stents.
howToTake: Take once daily at bedtime; do not stop without doctor's advice, especially the antiplatelet component.

description: Combines a statin for cholesterol control with low-dose aspirin for antiplatelet protection — reduces pill burden in cardiac patients.
howToTake: Take once daily at bedtime with or without food; report any muscle pain to your doctor.

description: Combines a beta-blocker and calcium channel blocker for blood pressure and heart rate control. Useful in hypertension with angina.
howToTake: Take once daily with food; do not stop abruptly.

description: A combination of an ACE inhibitor and diuretic for blood pressure control when single agents are insufficient.
howToTake: Take once daily in the morning; monitor potassium and kidney function.

description: A triple combination for resistant hypertension: ARB + calcium channel blocker + diuretic provides comprehensive BP control through three mechanisms.
howToTake: Take once daily in the morning; regular electrolyte and kidney function monitoring recommended.

description: A dual-action combination for enlarged prostate: tamsulosin provides quick symptomatic relief while dutasteride shrinks the prostate over time.
howToTake: Take once daily 30 minutes after the same meal each day; swallow capsules whole.

description: A combination for mixed infections: levofloxacin covers aerobic bacteria while ornidazole handles anaerobic bacteria and parasites.
howToTake: Take twice daily after meals; avoid alcohol during treatment.

description: Combines acid suppression with levosulpiride's prokinetic and anti-anxiety action for functional dyspepsia and GERD with anxiety-related symptoms.
howToTake: Take 30 minutes before meals; avoid in patients with Parkinson's disease.

description: An antimicrobial similar to metronidazole but with a longer half-life, used for amoebic dysentery, giardiasis, and bacterial vaginosis.
howToTake: Take with food to reduce nausea; avoid alcohol during and for 3 days after treatment.

description: An antiprotozoal and antibacterial for amoebic infections, giardiasis, and anaerobic bacterial infections. Better tolerated than metronidazole.
howToTake: Take after meals; complete the full course even if symptoms improve early.

description: A combination inhaler with a corticosteroid (fluticasone) and long-acting bronchodilator (formoterol) for maintenance asthma and COPD treatment.
howToTake: Inhale twice daily; rinse mouth after each use. Not for acute rescue — use salbutamol for sudden attacks.

description: The R-isomer of salbutamol — provides bronchodilation with fewer tremors and palpitations. Used for asthma and COPD rescue.
howToTake: Use as rescue inhaler or via nebuliser; 1-2 puffs every 4-6 hours as needed.

description: A highly selective beta-1 blocker with additional vasodilating properties via nitric oxide. Better tolerated than older beta-blockers with less fatigue and sexual side effects.
howToTake: Take once daily with or without food; do not stop abruptly.

description: Combines nerve pain medication with nerve-nourishing vitamin B12 for diabetic neuropathy and other nerve pain conditions.
howToTake: Take 2-3 times daily with food; dose is gradually increased based on response.

description: The gold standard treatment for Parkinson's disease. Levodopa converts to dopamine in the brain; carbidopa prevents its premature conversion outside the brain.
howToTake: Take 30 minutes before meals for best absorption; take at evenly spaced intervals throughout the day.

description: A stimulant laxative that acts on the colon to promote bowel movements. Commonly used for occasional constipation and bowel preparation.
howToTake: Take at bedtime; effect occurs within 6-12 hours. Not for daily long-term use.

description: An osmotic laxative that draws water into the stool for softer, easier bowel movements. Safe for regular use and preferred over stimulant laxatives.
howToTake: Dissolve one sachet in a glass of water; take once daily, usually in the morning. Drink plenty of fluids.

description: Combines acid suppression with itopride's prokinetic action for GERD with impaired gastric motility — bloating, early satiety, and postprandial fullness.
howToTake: Take 30 minutes before meals; swallow capsule whole.

description: A prokinetic that speeds up stomach emptying without the cardiac risks of domperidone. Used for functional dyspepsia, bloating, and gastroparesis.
howToTake: Take 30 minutes before meals, 3 times daily.

genericName: Mecobalamin + Alpha Lipoic Acid + Vitamin D3 + Folic Acid + Pyridoxine
description: A comprehensive nerve health supplement combining B12, ALA, D3, folic acid, and B6 for diabetic neuropathy and general nerve support.
howToTake: Take once daily after food; commonly prescribed alongside diabetes medications.

description: A combination of calcium channel blocker and ARB for blood pressure control. Amlodipine provides strong BP reduction while losartan adds kidney protection.
howToTake: Take once daily with or without food at a consistent time.

description: A well-established combination of beta-blocker and diuretic for hypertension. Provides synergistic blood pressure lowering.
howToTake: Take once daily in the morning; monitor potassium levels periodically.

description: A premium combination providing glucose-lowering via kidney excretion (empagliflozin) and reduced liver glucose production (metformin), with proven heart and kidney benefits.
howToTake: Take twice daily with meals; drink plenty of water. Maintain good hygiene to prevent genital infections.

description: A triple diabetes combination for patients needing intensive blood sugar control: DPP-4 inhibitor + metformin + sulfonylurea.
howToTake: Take with the first main meal of the day; monitor blood sugar regularly for hypoglycemia.

description: A modern ARB + diuretic combination for hypertension. Chlorthalidone provides stronger and longer-lasting blood pressure control than HCTZ.
howToTake: Take once daily in the morning; monitor potassium and kidney function periodically.

description: The standard triple therapy for heart failure with reduced ejection fraction: ARNI + beta-blocker. Sacubitril/valsartan + metoprolol is the current guideline recommendation.
howToTake: Sacubitril/valsartan twice daily, metoprolol once daily; doses titrated up gradually under medical supervision.

description: A triple calcium channel blocker (L/T/N type) that lowers BP with less ankle swelling and reflex tachycardia. Also provides kidney protection in diabetic patients.
howToTake: Take once daily in the morning with or without food.

description: An anti-osteoarthritis drug that works by inhibiting interleukin-1, reducing joint inflammation and slowing cartilage breakdown. Takes 2-4 weeks to show effect.
howToTake: Take once daily initially, increasing to twice daily after 2-4 weeks; take with food.

description: A joint health supplement believed to support cartilage repair and reduce osteoarthritis symptoms. Evidence is mixed but widely used.
howToTake: Take 1-2 tablets daily with food; may take 4-8 weeks to notice improvement.

description: A triple diabetes combination providing three complementary mechanisms: glucose excretion via kidneys, reduced liver glucose production, and increased insulin release.
howToTake: Take once daily with a meal; drink plenty of water throughout the day.

description: Combines a steroid with paracetamol for conditions needing anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects together, like severe allergic reactions and dental procedures.
howToTake: Take after meals as directed; use for the shortest duration possible.

description: A triple combination cream with steroid (inflammation), antibiotic (bacteria), and antifungal (fungus) for mixed skin infections with inflammation.
howToTake: Apply a thin layer twice daily to affected area; do not use on face. Limit use to 2 weeks maximum.

description: An antifungal shampoo for dandruff, seborrheic dermatitis, and tinea versicolor of the scalp. Also helps with fungal-related hair loss.
howToTake: Apply to wet hair, lather, leave for 3-5 minutes, then rinse. Use 2-3 times per week for treatment, once weekly for maintenance.

description: A newer topical antifungal that is highly effective against dermatophytes (ringworm, athlete's foot). Works with shorter treatment courses than older antifungals.
howToTake: Apply once daily at bedtime to clean, dry affected skin for 1-2 weeks.

description: Combines two nerve pain medications with different mechanisms for severe neuropathic pain that doesn't respond to either drug alone.
howToTake: Take at bedtime (both cause drowsiness); dose is adjusted gradually by your doctor.

description: A triple BP combination for resistant hypertension: ARB + CCB + diuretic provides comprehensive blood pressure control through three different mechanisms.
howToTake: Take once daily in the morning; monitor electrolytes and kidney function regularly.

description: A loop diuretic with longer duration and more predictable absorption than furosemide. Increasingly preferred in heart failure management.
howToTake: Take once daily in the morning; monitor potassium levels and kidney function.

description: A potent corticosteroid with less sodium retention than prednisolone. Used for severe inflammation, autoimmune conditions, and acute flares.
howToTake: Take with food in the morning; dose tapered gradually — never stop abruptly after prolonged use.

description: An immunosuppressant used for autoimmune diseases (lupus, rheumatoid arthritis, IBD) and to prevent organ transplant rejection.
howToTake: Take with food to reduce nausea; regular blood count monitoring is essential.

description: A potent immunosuppressant used for organ transplant rejection prevention and severe autoimmune diseases like lupus nephritis.
howToTake: Take twice daily on an empty stomach; do not crush or open capsules. Regular blood monitoring required.

description: A combination of ARB and beta-blocker for patients needing both blood pressure lowering and heart rate control.
howToTake: Take once daily with food; do not stop abruptly.

description: A combination of metformin and sulfonylurea gliclazide for Type 2 diabetes.
howToTake: Take with the first meal of the day.

description: A dual combination SGLT2+DPP4 inhibitor for diabetes with kidney-safe dosing.
howToTake: Take once daily in the morning with or without food.

description: A triple diabetes combination pill for patients needing three oral antidiabetics.
howToTake: Take once daily with the first meal.

description: A combination statin + fibrate for mixed dyslipidemia with both high LDL and high triglycerides.
howToTake: Take once daily with dinner.

description: A loop diuretic with longer action than furosemide, preferred for heart failure due to more predictable absorption.
howToTake: Take once daily in the morning.

description: A selective aldosterone antagonist for heart failure post-MI without the hormonal side effects of spironolactone.
howToTake: Take once daily with or without food; monitor potassium levels.

description: A direct vasodilator for resistant hypertension; commonly used with isosorbide dinitrate in heart failure (BiDil concept).
howToTake: Take 2–4 times daily with food.

description: A newer antihypertensive combination with less ankle swelling than amlodipine-based combinations.
howToTake: Take once daily.

description: A fourth-generation fluoroquinolone for respiratory infections, sinusitis, and complicated skin infections.
howToTake: Take once daily with or without food; complete the full course.

description: A macrolide antibiotic for respiratory and skin infections with fewer GI side effects than erythromycin.
howToTake: Take on an empty stomach 15 minutes before food, twice daily.

description: A macrolide antibiotic and prokinetic agent; also used for acne and as erythromycin base for GI motility.
howToTake: Take on an empty stomach 1 hour before food; or with food if stomach upset occurs.

description: A tetracycline antibiotic commonly used for moderate-to-severe acne, RA, and MRSA skin infections.
howToTake: Take twice daily with food; less photosensitivity than doxycycline.

description: A third-generation oral cephalosporin for sinusitis, pharyngitis, and skin infections.
howToTake: Take once or twice daily with or without food; iron supplements may turn stool red (harmless).

description: A hemorheological agent that improves blood flow by reducing blood viscosity; used for peripheral vascular disease.
howToTake: Take 2–3 times daily with meals; swallow whole.

description: A newer non-sedating antihistamine for allergic rhinitis and urticaria without drowsiness or cardiac effects.
howToTake: Take once daily on an empty stomach, 1 hour before or 2 hours after food.

description: The active metabolite of loratadine with slightly faster onset and non-sedating properties.
howToTake: Take once daily with or without food.

description: A dual antihistamine and PAF antagonist for allergic rhinitis and chronic urticaria with non-sedating profile.
howToTake: Take once daily with or without food.

description: An atypical antipsychotic for schizophrenia and bipolar depression with minimal weight gain and metabolic effects.
howToTake: Take once daily with at least 350 calories of food for absorption.

description: An atypical antipsychotic with antidepressant properties at low doses, used for schizophrenia and dysthymia.
howToTake: Take once or twice daily with or without food.

description: A combination heart failure therapy: beta-blocker with sinus node inhibitor for optimized heart rate control.
howToTake: Take twice daily with food.

description: A chloride channel activator for chronic idiopathic constipation and IBS with constipation.
howToTake: Take twice daily with food and water; swallow whole.

description: An osmotic laxative and antacid for occasional constipation and acid indigestion.
howToTake: Take 15–30ml at bedtime with a full glass of water.

description: A natural stimulant laxative derived from senna plant for occasional constipation.
howToTake: Take at bedtime for morning effect; drink plenty of water. Do not use for more than 1 week.

description: An antispasmodic that relaxes gut smooth muscle for colic, IBS, and renal/biliary spasm.
howToTake: Take 3–4 times daily as needed before meals.

description: Fixed-dose combination for the continuation phase of TB treatment (months 3–6).
howToTake: Take on an empty stomach; dose based on body weight.

description: A potassium binder for treating hyperkalemia in kidney disease; exchanges calcium for potassium in the gut.
howToTake: Mix in water and drink; take separately from other medications by 3 hours.

description: An alkalinizing agent for metabolic acidosis in kidney disease and as an antacid for heartburn.
howToTake: Take with water as directed; for chronic use in CKD, take with meals.

description: A magnesium supplement for deficiency, muscle cramps, and constipation; also used in migraine prevention.
howToTake: Take once or twice daily with food.

description: An antiseptic mouthwash for gingivitis, mouth ulcers, and post-dental procedure care.
howToTake: Rinse with 15ml for 30 seconds twice daily after brushing; do not swallow.

description: An anti-inflammatory mouthwash/gargle for sore throat, mouth ulcers, and post-radiation mucositis.
howToTake: Rinse or gargle with 15ml every 1.5–3 hours; do not swallow.

description: A broad-spectrum antiseptic for wound cleaning, surgical preparation, and treating minor skin infections.
howToTake: Apply to affected area 1–3 times daily; dilute for mouthwash use. For external use only.

description: A topical antifungal for dermatophytosis, cutaneous candidiasis, and tinea versicolor.
howToTake: Apply twice daily to affected area for 2–4 weeks.

description: A potent topical corticosteroid for eczema, psoriasis, and inflammatory skin conditions with once-daily dosing.
howToTake: Apply a thin layer once daily; do not use on face for more than 5 days.

description: A topical antibiotic for inflammatory acne; kills Cutibacterium acnes bacteria on the skin.
howToTake: Apply a thin layer to affected areas once or twice daily after washing face.

description: A topical for acne and rosacea that reduces inflammation, kills bacteria, and fades post-acne marks.
howToTake: Apply twice daily to clean skin; takes 4–6 weeks for visible improvement.

description: A topical retinoid for acne and photoaging that promotes cell turnover and reduces comedones.
howToTake: Apply a pea-sized amount to clean, dry skin at bedtime; use sunscreen daily. Expect peeling initially.

description: A combination glaucoma eye drop with prostaglandin analog and beta-blocker for superior IOP control.
howToTake: Instill 1 drop in the affected eye once daily at bedtime.

description: A prostaglandin analog for glaucoma and ocular hypertension with preservative-free options available.
howToTake: Instill 1 drop in the affected eye once daily at bedtime.

description: An injectable iron preparation for severe iron deficiency anemia when oral iron fails or is not tolerated.
howToTake: Given as slow IV infusion in hospital/clinic; total dose calculated based on hemoglobin deficit.

description: A hormone that stimulates red blood cell production, used for anemia in chronic kidney disease and chemotherapy.
howToTake: Given as subcutaneous or IV injection 1–3 times weekly; monitor hemoglobin and iron levels.

description: A growth factor that stimulates white blood cell production, used after chemotherapy to reduce infection risk.
howToTake: Given as subcutaneous injection daily, starting 24 hours after chemotherapy; continue until WBC recovers.

description: An intravenous anticoagulant for acute PE, DVT, cardiac surgery, and dialysis; requires aPTT monitoring.
howToTake: Given as continuous IV infusion or intermittent subcutaneous injection; requires frequent monitoring.

description: A combination bronchodilator and mucolytic commonly prescribed for COPD and asthma with productive cough.
howToTake: Take 2–3 times daily after meals.

description: A combination bronchodilator nebulization solution for acute bronchospasm in asthma and COPD exacerbations.
howToTake: Use via nebulizer 3–4 times daily during exacerbations.

description: An inhaled corticosteroid for nebulizer use in young children with asthma and acute croup.
howToTake: Use via nebulizer once or twice daily; rinse child's face after use.

description: A combination nebulization solution with bronchodilator and steroid for pediatric asthma exacerbations.
howToTake: Use via nebulizer as directed by physician.

description: A selective estrogen receptor modulator for hormone-receptor-positive breast cancer treatment and prevention.
howToTake: Take once daily with or without food; typically used for 5–10 years.

description: An aromatase inhibitor for postmenopausal hormone-receptor-positive breast cancer.
howToTake: Take once daily with or without food.

description: An oral chemotherapy drug for breast, colon, and gastric cancer; converted to 5-FU in tumor cells.
howToTake: Take twice daily within 30 minutes after meals for 14 days, then 7 days off.

description: A tyrosine kinase inhibitor for CML and GIST; a breakthrough targeted cancer therapy.
howToTake: Take once daily with a meal and a large glass of water.

description: An oral disease-modifying therapy for relapsing multiple sclerosis.
howToTake: Take twice daily with food; start at 120mg and increase to 240mg after 7 days.

description: A mast cell stabilizer eye drop for allergic conjunctivitis prevention; takes a few days for full effect.
howToTake: Instill 1–2 drops in each eye 4–6 times daily; continue throughout allergy season.

description: A dual-action antihistamine and mast cell stabilizer eye drop for allergic conjunctivitis.
howToTake: Instill 1 drop in each eye twice daily (0.1%) or once daily (0.2%).
